summary.intent: Implement the agnostic core of the Highlight utility component (task #204) for ars-ui, then run thorough spec-drift, iterative improvement, and test coverage audits, and codify the improvement process as a reusable skill.
summary.outcome: Delivered a complete, production-quality Highlight module with TR21 Unicode case folding, three match strategies, multi-query range merging, 41 unit tests, 9 insta snapshots, proptest invariants, spec-conformance tests, and 0 missed mutations. Discovered and fixed a silent German eszett contract violation (ß↔ss required case folding, not lowercasing). Created the post-implementation-audit skill and registered it as a mandatory CLAUDE.md step.

repo: ars-components declares ars-i18n = { default-features = false }, so ICU4X features must be explicitly propagated via a new feature flag (e.g., i18n = ["ars-i18n/icu4x"]) — they are not inherited automatically.

ars_i18n::to_lowercase wraps CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string, which PRESERVES ß rather than expanding it to 'ss'. TR21 case folding (ß↔ss, Greek final-sigma collapse) requires a separate case_fold helper using CaseMapper::fold_string.

cargo llvm-cov does not cover wasm-gated code paths; adapter crates (ars-leptos, ars-dioxus) require cargo xtask coverage wasm --package <crate> --feature <f> --file out.lcov.info. The correct flag is --feature (repeatable), NOT --features.
CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md; .claude/skills/ is a symlink to .agents/skills/. The canonical path for skills is .agents/skills/.

ConnectApi::part_attrs takes a Part by value with no payload, making 'parametric anatomy slots' (parts whose attributes depend on runtime data, like highlighted: bool) impossible to represent as Part enum variants — they must be exposed as parameterised Api methods instead.
The workspace cargo-mutants configuration lives in .cargo/mutants.toml and supports skip entries with justification comments to document equivalent mutations without disabling the test suite.

Adapter Cargo.toml files must explicitly wire feature propagation: icu4x = ["ars-components/i18n"] in ars-leptos and ars-dioxus, otherwise Highlight is invisible to adapter builds even when icu4x is enabled.
cargo xtask coverage check-all --file <lcov> is the CI threshold gate, not bare cargo llvm-cov. Bare llvm-cov reports 0% for wasm-only paths, creating false gaps.

code.finding: Uses CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string (via ars_i18n::to_lowercase) for case-insensitive matching, which does NOT expand ß to ss. This was the precedent cited for Highlight's initial approach — but it is semantically wrong for TR21 case-insensitive matching.

to_lowercase is a locale-aware wrapper around CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string. A new case_fold function was added wrapping CaseMapper::fold_turkic_string / fold_string to correctly implement TR21 equivalence.

Dead continue branch guarding against empty case_fold output — unreachable because TR21 guarantees every assigned character folds to a non-empty replacement. Was removed to achieve 100% region coverage without misleading future readers.
merge_ranges 'contained range' else branch (incoming range fully inside an existing range → no-op) had zero test coverage. Required a dedicated test: query=["foobar","ooba"] on text="foobar".

Added i18n = ["ars-i18n/icu4x"] as a non-default feature. Highlight module is gated on #[cfg(feature = "i18n")] — no fallback path. Agnostic-core is absent from builds without i18n rather than silently falling back to ASCII lowercasing.

workflow: The post-implementation-audit 3-phase flow (spec-drift → iterative 'anything else?' × 2 clean rounds → test coverage) collapsed what would have been 3+ user-driven review rounds into a single pre-PR pass. Codified as .agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md.

Mutation testing (cargo mutants) catches test gaps that line coverage misses: the Api::ignore_case accessor could be hardcoded to false with 98.67% line coverage still passing — mutation testing caught it immediately.
TDD ordering matters: writing tests before implementation for utility components prevents the trap of writing tests that confirm existing behavior rather than specifying intended behavior.

Spec drift audits should recommend 'best outcome independent of either side' — not 'spec wins' or 'impl wins'. Three categories emerged: spec catches up to impl (most common), impl changes to match spec intent (dir=auto in root_attrs, case folding switch), and a third design different from both (not needed here but the rubric should remain open to it).

Adapter specs (leptos, dioxus) need to be updated in the same PR as the agnostic core. They hardcoded attribute strings and described the wrong API surface — a separate PR would have let adapter implementations diverge from the updated core.

Skill descriptions should use 'any of these phrases' (disjunctive) not 'phrases like' to prevent ambiguity about trigger conditions. Skills also need a Quick flow TL;DR section for long workflow skills so the model orients before reading the dense body.

When both spec and impl are wrong (not just drifted), escalate to the user via AskUserQuestion rather than unilaterally picking a direction — the eszett case was the concrete example where the original plan recommendation was wrong.

friction: Plan stated 'no Cargo.toml changes' based on an unverified assumption about feature inheritance. The assumption was wrong — ars-i18n/icu4x is not transitively enabled for ars-components. Should have verified cargo metadata before writing the plan.

ars_i18n::to_lowercase was incorrectly described as satisfying the German eszett ß↔ss contract during the initial case-folding tradeoff analysis. The bug was silent: the original German test passed by using STRASSE (uppercase-only) where lowercasing trivially works, masking the real failure on mixed-case Straße.
cargo xci completed without a notification reaching the assistant, causing a polling wait instead of automatic continuation.
The cargo xtask coverage wasm flag names were wrong in the initial skill draft (--features and --output instead of --feature repeatable and --file). Required a verify step against --help output.

Two stale 'ars_i18n::to_lowercase' doc references in highlight.rs survived all audit rounds and were only caught immediately before commit. The switch from to_lowercase to case_fold was not systematically searched in doc comments, only in code.
Skill file path was initially written to .claude/skills/ (the symlink) rather than .agents/skills/ (canonical). AGENTS.md also initially referenced the wrong path.
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter wrappers for Highlight (rendering HighlightChunk as <mark>/<span>) — explicitly out of scope for task #204, lands with separate adapter tasks.
Prelude re-exports for Highlight in ars-leptos and ars-dioxus — deferred to adapter implementation tasks.

Promoting the 'parametric anatomy slot' concept more fully in foundation/10-component-spec-template.md with examples from tables, tags-input, and toaster (which will all have the same per-row/per-item parametric part pattern).

Performance optimization of per-char allocation in build_normalised — ICU4X 2.x has no streaming case-fold API, so the current per-char String allocation is the only correct approach until upstream provides one.
Broadening proptest to cover the Props builder pattern (Props::new().query([]).text('').ignore_case(false).match_strategy(Fuzzy)) and verify builder ergonomics are fuzz-stable.
